entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop that can tolerate high temperatures, drought, and low-fertility conditions.

entities.name: HIGH TEMPERATURES
entities.description: Environmental stress that pearl millet can tolerate.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Environmental stress that pearl millet can tolerate and which downregulates PGWRKY74.

entities.name: LOW-FERTILITY CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental stress that pearl millet can tolerate.

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTION FACTORS
entities.description: Proteins that regulate transcription of target genes and downstream biological processes.

entities.name: PGWRKY74
entities.description: A group IIc WRKY transcription factor gene in pearl millet involved in drought tolerance.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain productivity under drought stress.

entities.name: YEAST ONE-HYBRID
entities.description: An assay used to examine transcriptional activation potential.

entities.name: GEL SHIFT ASSAYS
entities.description: An assay used to examine DNA-binding ability.

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTIONAL ACTIVATION POTENTIAL
entities.description: The capacity of a transcription factor to activate gene expression.

entities.name: DNA-BINDING ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of a protein to bind to specific DNA motifs.

entities.name: ARABIDOPSIS THALIANA
entities.description: A model plant used to test the effects of overexpressing PGWRKY74.

entities.name: MANNITOL
entities.description: A substance used to simulate osmotic stress/drought in plants.

entities.name: NACL
entities.description: Sodium chloride used to simulate salinity stress in plants.

entities.name: RD29B
entities.description: A stress-responsive gene induced by abscisic acid.

entities.name: ABSCISIC ACID
entities.description: A stress-related phytohormone that induces the expression of RD29B.

entities.name: SHOOT GROWTH
entities.description: The growth of the plant's above-ground parts, which is retarded by PGWRKY74 overexpression under stress.
